target.name: Pickle Robot Company
target.founder: AJ Meyer, Ariana Eisenstein, Dan Paluska

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $97M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $1.0B - $1.8B.

private.name: Bedrock Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.75B

private.name: Dexterity
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.65B

private.name: The Bot Company
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B)

data.narrative:
Pickle Robot Company is a Series B-stage robotics and manufacturing firm that has raised $97M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 9 comparable companies across the robotics and manufacturing sector—1 public and 8 private firms including Bedrock Robotics, Dexterity, Divergent, Exotec, and Locus Robotics, among others.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $97M total raised as a proxy base value and applies a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B-to-next-stage expansion, yielding $184M. Peer-median analysis examines four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables: Bedrock Robotics at $1.8B (Series B), Dexterity at $1.6B (Series B), Hadrian at $1.6B (Series C), and Nimble Robotics at $1.0B (Series C). The median across this set is $1.6B, with a range of $1.0B to $1.8B.

Triangulation across both methods supports a valuation range of $157M (low) to $1.9B (high), with a median of $905M. The wide spread reflects material tension between backward-looking capital raised and forward-looking peer comparables, suggesting stage and operational performance assumptions are critical to narrowing the range. Key uncertainties include Pickle's relative traction versus named peers and whether the peer set's valuations reflect recent market repricing in frontier robotics.
